pages.full_text:
cold. The van heater is broken. We had a good supper in the local pub, with a roaring fire.

    We asked how much it cost at the campsite & the farmer said £3 a night & John thought he said "£3 and 8".

[photo: The latrines at Housesteads, as they were] [caption written vertically along right edge: The latrines at Housesteads, as they were]

[text continues bottom-right, partially cut off or continued on next spread]
photo_captions: The latrines at Housesteads, as they were
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation from the previous spread. The photo shows the actual Roman latrines at Housesteads (an archaeological site on Hadrian's Wall). Caption is written vertically along the right margin. Bottom-right text is cut off at the gutter/edge and continues on next page.

pages.full_text:
[small partial text visible at top-left gutter from left page: ...ra / the / [illegible]]

[postcard/illustration: artist's reconstruction of Romans using the latrines at Housesteads]

    The sun shone in the morning. Toni got filthy digging his [?own] way into rabbit holes.

    We went to __Housesteads__, I remembered the loos.
pages.notes: Page number 10 visible in top-right corner. Toni appears to be a dog (digging into rabbit holes). The illustration is a printed postcard showing a colourful artist's reconstruction of Romans using the communal latrines at Housesteads fort, Hadrian's Wall. The word 'his' is followed by a crossed-out word (possibly 'own' or a dog's name) before 'way'. Year cannot be confirmed from text alone; volume covers 2003-2019, position near start of volume may suggest early in that range but this is not asserted.
